channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: More than a dozen Jewish groups say they're leaving X amid Musk controversies
item.description: More than a dozen U.S. and Canadian Jewish groups said Tuesday they will leave the social media platform X, stating it "has become rife with toxic speech" amid Elon Musk's changes to the popular social networking app. "More than a dozen U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Linux gets support for the Copilot key
item.description: There is a special Copilot key on some Windows laptops that can be used to launch Microsoft’s AI assistant — and now support for the key is coming to Linux.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Don't panic! Baldur's Gate 3 players on PS5 who were affected by the accidental release of Patch 8 can fix their game using this easy solution
item.description: Baldur's Gate 3 Patch 8 was accidentally dropped early for PS5 players, but thankfully there's a way to rescue your save file.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: It's looking bleak for AMD's older GPUs - a file spotted in unofficial Radeon drivers further hints at FSR 4's RDNA 4 exclusivity
item.description: It doesn't look very good for anyone who may have hoped FSR 4 would find its way to older GPUs, as a DLL file in unofficial Radeon drivers suggest otherwise.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Fake toll road texts sweep America as Chinese scammers target US drivers
item.description: Toll road text scam: Fake messages claim unpaid fees, seek payment via fraudulent links. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says this scam is becoming increasingly sophisticated and widespread.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Vatican document offers AI guidelines from warfare to health care
item.description: A Vatican document released Tuesday offers wide-ranging ethical guidelines for the application of artificial intelligence in sectors from warfare to health care The underlying call was that the burgeoning technology must be used to complement, and not replace, human intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: OpenAI announces new ChatGPT version for government agencies
item.description: OpenAI is launching a new version of its popular ChatGPT model that is specifically tailored to government agencies and workers as the artificial intelligence (AI) firm seeks to remain a top player in the AI space. The product, dubbed “ChatGPT Gov,” will give workers at U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Philips' new OLED TVs offer next-gen panels with your choice of extra image quality or better built-in sound, and I like this direction
item.description: LG's new Primary RGB Tandem panels, combined with hardcore image processing and Bowers & Wilkins sound options.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The Bear’s Jeremy Allen White will star in Netflix’s new show from the author of Call Me By Your Name, and I need a streaming date immediately
item.description: Another André Aciman novel is coming to the screen, this time for a Netflix limited series starring Jeremy Allen White as the lead role.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Upstart Chinese AI company DeepSeek's founder started out as a low-key hedge fund entrepreneur
item.description: The 40-year-old founder of China's DeepSeek, an AI startup that has startled markets with its capacity to compete with industry leaders like OpenAI, kept a low profile as he built up a hedge fund that now manages a reported $8 billion in assets.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How to prioritize AI initiatives: A strategic framework for maximizing ROI
item.description: “There are only two types of companies in this world, those who are great at AI and everybody else. If you don’t know AI, you are going to fail, period, end of story. You have to understand it, because it will have a significant impact on every single thing that you do. There’s no avoiding it.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: IT predictions: 5 things that won’t change in 2025
item.description: Every January futurists opine on what will be different. Just as important, but typically underappreciated, are trends that will perpetuate themselves deep into the year to come. Here’s a look at what I see staying the same for IT leaders in 2025.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: At least 11 Baltic cables have been damaged in 15 months, prompting NATO to up its guard
item.description: NATO is deploying eyes in the sky and on the Baltic Sea to protect cables and pipelines that stitch together the nine countries with shores on Baltic waters.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester And Emma Burrows, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says Microsoft is one of the companies eyeing TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday evening that Microsoft is among the U.S. companies looking to take control of TikTok to help the popular app avert an effective ban that could kick-in in April.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: After alarming audit findings, here’s how HHS can overcome cloud security gaps
item.description: The report highlights the possibility of unauthorized access and control over critical systems. The post After alarming audit findings, here’s how HHS can overcome cloud security gaps first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Japanese EV automaker ZM Trucks plants its US base in California
item.description: The company said it will use that facility to manufacture a range of zero-emission products including electric commercial trucks, tractors, and airport ground service equipment.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Gowen, The Mercury News

item.title: Businesses get their own version of the Chrome Web Store
item.description: Though there are a variety of cool extensions for the Chrome browser, there are also malicious extensions that pose a security threat. To increase security, Google has now launched the Chrome Web Store for Enterprises, a new store specifically designed for business users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Outsmart hackers who are out to steal your identity
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers tips on how to protect your identity: Be vigilant, monitor accounts, use smart security, know theft response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: High-speed Ethernet switches a bright spot in network forecasts
item.description: Switches and routers remain the workhorses of the networking world. Viewed through that lens, 2024 offered a mixed bag of results for the networking market. There were numerous areas of decline, particularly in the enterprise market, according to some analysts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Sweden seizes vessel suspected of 'sabotage' after undersea data cable rupture in Baltic Sea
item.description: RIGA, Latvia (AP) — Swedish prosecutors announced Sunday night that they have opened a preliminary investigation into suspected aggravated “sabotage” and ordered the detention of a vessel in the Baltic Sea suspected of damaging an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: FortiGate admins advised to run a compromise assessment
item.description: Network administrators with Fortinet’s FortiGate next generation firewall in their IT environments are being warned to thoroughly scrutinize systems for possible compromise, following last week’s dump of stolen configuration and VPN credentials by a threat actor.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pakistan's parliament passes bill with sweeping controls on social media
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistan’s lower house of parliament on Thursday passed a controversial bill that will give the government sweeping controls on social media, including sending users to prison for spreading disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk clashes with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over Trump-supported Stargate AI data center project
item.description: Elon Musk is clashing with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman over the Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project touted by President Donald Trump, the latest in a feud between the two tech billionaires that started on OpenAI's board and is now testing Musk's influence with the new president.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn't in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star. Donaldson stirred interest in a Jan. 13 post on X saying he’d “buy TikTok so it doesn’t get banned.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of setting fire to congressman's office over TikTok ban charged with arson
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Prosecutors charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman's office because he was upset with the federal TikTok ban with multiple counts on Wednesday, including arson.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, NewsNation
